title: carmichaelia williamsii
intro: Carmichaelia williamsii, also known as the New Zealand spinach or karamea, is a flowering legume native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Carmichaelia williamsii include the potential to reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of fever, as well as its use as a diuretic to aid in the treatment of urinary tract infections. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves, which can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules for medicinal use.
